rules.name: no_vet_villain
rules.description: Never make the vet the villain. The system is the problem, not the local vet. Damages the clinic network trust.
rules.rationale: VetPartners owns the clinics. Making vets look bad undermines the network that delivers the product.

rules.name: no_time_bound_claims
rules.description: No time-bound claims that break when the ad runs past the date. Use visit-count framing instead.
rules.rationale: Evergreen ads must not contain time-sensitive language. 'Before Easter' shown in May breaks trust.

rules.name: no_geographic_specificity
rules.description: No city or region names in ad copy. Geo-targeting is handled at ad set level, not in creative.
rules.rationale: Ads are geo-targeted at platform level. City names in copy limit creative reuse and can feel invasive.

rules.name: clinic_count_current
rules.description: Block outdated clinic count '190+ clinics'. Current approved figure is '270+ clinics'.
rules.rationale: Clinic count updated from 190+ to 270+. Outdated figures understate the network and contradict current marketing materials.

rules.name: low_risk_trio_present
rules.description: Body copy must contain all three low-risk signals: 'no joining fee' AND 'no waiting period' AND 'cancel anytime'. These pre-empt the top three objections.
rules.rationale: The low-risk trio was distilled from 6 review cycles. Omitting any one leaves a major objection unaddressed. Always close with all three together.

rules.name: not_insurance_disclaimer
rules.description: Every ad must contain a 'not insurance' or equivalent disclaimer. Failure to distinguish from insurance is misleading.
rules.rationale: Best for Pet is a wellness plan, NOT insurance. Misidentification risks consumer confusion and regulatory issues under Australian Consumer Law.

rules.name: no_subscription_language
rules.description: Avoid 'subscription' and 'subscribe'. Use 'plan' or 'membership' instead to avoid subscription fatigue.
rules.rationale: Consumer research shows subscription fatigue is real. 'Plan' and 'membership' tested better. 'One monthly fee' is the preferred framing.

rules.name: no_guilt_piling
rules.description: Do not pile guilt on the audience. They already feel guilty about delayed care (74% report guilt). Offer relief, not more shame.
rules.rationale: 100% of pet parents report guilt. 74% felt guilty after delaying care. The audience needs the barrier removed, not more shame. Relief > guilt.
